text: Welcome to This is Markitecture. I'm Lana from AWS and today we're talking with Tim from Zixi.
Hi Tim. Hi, thanks for having me. Great to have you here. So what does Zixi do? So Zixi provides
a software platform for transporting broadcast quality video over the public internet or other
IP networks. So how does the broadcast work and how are you using elemental services as a part of
it? Sure. So imagine you need to get video from a sports stadium, you know, many camera angles up to
the cloud for processing. So what what Zixi is doing is basically you take your raw video from cameras
over to elemental live encoders, for instance, which has Zixi software built in. And then from
the elemental live encoders through a network connection that's connected to the internet,
the stream goes up to the cloud. So this could be elemental media connect services or it could be
Zixi broadcasters in the cloud. And so this is what this is doing is allowing you to contribute that
video from on premise up to the cloud, but completely using internet based, you know, resources, public
internet, instead of using satellite. Great. So I want to talk today about the architecture of the
control plane itself. So the services on their own are encoding the data, but how do I manage
configuration and latency and other aspects of streaming? Great question. So here we were talking
about, you know, what the data flow, which is the video. So, you know, the users need to be able to
control all of that remotely. So within Zen Master, which is our SAS based control plane, the user can remotely
from a web based interface, log into their elemental encoders that are on premise and do all the
configuration work. They also can create configurations for elemental media connect to receive those
streams from the camera and then send those streams back out. So all of this is, you know, it's done from,
you know, where remotely from within Zen Master, I'm controlling everything that's happening either on
premise or in the cloud. Great. So we have configuration done, and we have, you know,
we have the video stream that is being processed. So what kind of data are you storing in DynamoDB,
let's say, versus Elasticsearch? Right. So just to go back to configuration, so we use RDS for storing all
the configurations. Dynamo is really used for the real-time status. So, you know, imagine I have, you
know, thousands of different video workflows happening. You know, what's the current state of all the
different pieces? What's the state of the elemental encoders? What's the state of the media connect
flows? What's the state of, you know, Zixi broadcasters that are running in the cloud on EC2?
So this is where we're using Dynamo just to capture all of that real-time state. And then the user sees
that from within the Zen Master interface. They can see, you know, the state of everything, but also
when things, you know, if something goes into an error state, for instance, this is what allows us to
have an alerting system so that we can alert the user in real-time as well. Great. That something in
their workflow is not working. So you have real-time feedback to fix something that might be going
awry. Yep. And I'm also seeing that you're leveraging Elasticsearch. Yeah, absolutely. So Elastic is
really for long-term event storage. So, you know, maybe a couple weeks ago, you know, a customer has a
question about, you know, maybe one of their recipients of a stream said the stream went down.
So we can go into Elasticsearch and look at all the events that were happening in that time. And
maybe we discover that someone, you know, pulled the Ethernet cable out of an encoder. And so, of
course, at that point, that encoder can't send its stream up and it, you know, propagates down. But
with Elasticsearch and all the historical event logs there, we can look back in the past and figure out
what was happening at that time to, you know, to help the customer come up with a, you know,
an understanding of what happened. Great. Tim, thank you so much for telling us today about how you're
using Elemental Services and also how you've built this control plane that allows you to store
configurations, control latencies, and also provide real-time feedback to the users. And thank
you for watching. This is Markitecture.
